title: hibbertia basaltica
intro: Hibbertia basaltica, also known as the basalt guinea flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting potential anticancer properties. Hibbertia basaltica can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
